behaviors.description: Primary translator outputs a grammatically coherent, meaning-preserving translation (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real neural MT output quality (grammar/semantic preservation), which a stub cannot validate. Pass 2 challenge: Integration would only validate routing/payloads with fixed fixtures and would miss real model-quality failures (deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single model judgment, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated multilingual evaluation set through the real translation model API; score meaning preservation and fluency (e.g., bilingual human rubric or robust semantic adequacy checks) and fail if quality drops below threshold versus baseline.

behaviors.description: On API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not error to caller.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure is at module handoff (API client -> fallback selector -> dictionary translator -> response). Pass 2 challenge: not System, since behavior text is specifically 'returns a 503 error' and this can be reproduced with an in-process stubbed API response; no need for real network timing/OS effects. Not Agentic because correctness here is fallback orchestration, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a stub primary translator that returns 503, real fallback module (or realistic in-process fake), and assert successful response body comes from fallback plus no propagated error status.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is repo/service wiring with a local SQLite DB boundary. Pass 2 challenge: Unit is insufficient for this behavior text because correctness depends on actual DB lookup + mapping of stored phrase to returned translation, not only pure function logic. Rejected System because local DB/filesystem use is explicitly within Integration scope here and does not require external OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond normal module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite database with known phrase pairs, invoke fallback translator through service/repository path, and assert exact translation match for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because behavior includes both computation and persistence ('stores the metric'), so failure can occur at compute->storage handoff. Pass 2 challenge: Unit could catch edit-distance math alone, but does not cover storage wiring required by the behavior text. Rejected Agentic because metric correctness does not depend on model judgment quality; deterministic strings are sufficient. Rejected System because no real external infra beyond local module/DB interaction is required.
behaviors.test_description: Submit paired primary/fallback strings via service path, assert computed character-level edit distance equals expected value, and verify persisted metric record in test datastore.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is orchestration across modules (primary selection + background quality logging). Pass 2 challenge: not Agentic since assertion is about precedence and side-effect execution, not evaluating translation quality/content; stubs can expose orchestration bugs. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/process behavior; async background work can be validated in-process with controlled scheduler/worker hooks. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision is not under test and no multi-step agent pipeline is described.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators returning deterministic outputs, assert response uses primary output, then await/observe background task completion and verify quality-comparison log/metric was written.
